title: ClawChat: proper account system with email verification and token management
update_notes: R2: fold in Rocky's infrastructure check + redirect flow fix. ADMIN_TOKEN hashed on first boot. Verification redirect uses /?verified=1&token=<tok> query param so SPA can pick up token. Port 587 confirmed open to SendGrid. Blocker: SendGrid API key needed from jkh.
description: Build a real account/auth system for ClawChat replacing the current name-claim flow. Triggered by jkh feedback that auth gate is in the wrong place (mid-send prompt vs. pre-UI gate). The immediate UX fix (hide #app until login) shipped in a4c5665. This task covers the full account system.
blockers: SendGrid API key needed (SENDGRID_SMTP_PASS env var). jkh: do you have a SendGrid account, or should we create one? Outbound port 587 is confirmed open on do-host1.
requirements: Registration: POST /api/register with {username, email} — sends HTML verification email with one-time link
Email link /api/verify?token=<otp> → creates user, issues persistent token, redirects to /?verified=1&token=<issued_tok> so SPA can pick it up
SPA boot: detect ?verified=1&token=... param, save to localStorage, clear URL param — do NOT show /login again after verification
Login page (not modal): username + token fields, 'remember me' saves to localStorage (default on)
Forgot token / reset: enter email → triggers re-verification flow, invalidates ALL existing tokens for that user
Token shown exactly once after verification — user encouraged to save to password manager
On return visit with no saved token: show login page (username + token)
On 401 mid-session: non-intrusive banner (already shipped), 'sign in again' → login page
Email must be confirmed within 1 hour or the pending registration expires
All users have: id, username, email (verified), created_at, last_seen
Multi-device support: multiple active tokens per user (tokens table), new login does not revoke existing tokens

resolved_questions.smtp: SendGrid free tier (100/day) via lettre SMTP relay. Port 587 confirmed open on do-host1. No native outbound SMTP configured. Env vars: SENDGRID_SMTP_USER='apikey', SENDGRID_SMTP_PASS=<api_key>, SMTP_FROM. BLOCKER: key not yet in CCC secrets.
resolved_questions.multi_device_tokens: tokens table (id, user_id, token_hash, created_at, last_used_at). New login does NOT revoke existing tokens. Reset/forgot flow invalidates ALL tokens for that user. Scale is small so both per-device and single-token work — tokens table chosen for future-proofing at near-zero added complexity.
resolved_questions.username_mutable: Immutable after registration. @mention history and DM routing (dm-{a}-{b} canonical IDs) depend on stable usernames. Display name can be mutable later if needed.
resolved_questions.admin_bootstrap: ADMIN_TOKEN env var stays as permanent escape hatch. On first boot, hash it and store/compare the hash — consistent security model with user tokens, no plaintext comparison inline.
resolved_questions.verification_redirect: GET /api/verify redirects to /?verified=1&token=<issued_token>. SPA detects query params on boot, saves {username, token} to localStorage, clears URL. Avoids landing on /login with empty localStorage after clicking email link.

proposed_implementation.target_server: squirrelchat-server (Rust/Axum, :8793) — ALL auth logic lives here. Node/Express (:8790) proxies /api/register, /api/verify, /api/reset, /api/login through to :8793 like all other /api/* routes. No auth logic in :8790.
backend_changes: New DB table: pending_registrations(id, username, email, otp_hash, expires_at)
New DB table: tokens(id, user_id, token_hash, created_at, last_used_at) — replaces single token_hash on users
Alter users table: add email TEXT UNIQUE; username stays immutable; drop any plaintext token col
POST /api/register: validate username unique + email unique, insert pending_registration, send verification email via lettre + SendGrid SMTP relay
GET /api/verify?token=<otp>: look up pending, check expiry, create user row, generate sc-token, insert into tokens table, HTTP 302 redirect to /?verified=1&token=<issued_token>
POST /api/reset: look up email, DELETE all rows from tokens WHERE user_id = ..., insert new pending_registration, send email
POST /api/login: username + token → find matching token_hash in tokens table, update last_used_at, return user
ADMIN_TOKEN: on server startup, if ADMIN_TOKEN env var is set, hash it and compare against stored admin_token_hash — never compare plaintext inline
Auth middleware: validate Bearer token against tokens table on all protected routes
frontend_changes: Replace login modal with a proper /login route (full-page, not modal)
Add /reset route: email input → 'check your email' confirmation
Boot: check for ?verified=1&token=... query params first — if present, save to localStorage, clear URL, proceed authenticated
Boot fallback: if no localStorage session and no verified param → redirect to /login
localStorage: save {username, token} on 'remember me' checkbox (default on)
Express server (:8790) proxies /api/register, /api/verify, /api/reset, /api/login to :8793 — no auth logic in Express layer
email: lettre crate (Rust) with SendGrid SMTP relay (smtp.sendgrid.net:587)
Env vars: SENDGRID_SMTP_USER='apikey', SENDGRID_SMTP_PASS=<SendGrid API key>, SMTP_FROM=noreply@squirrelchat.jordanhubbard.net
HTML email template: ClawChat branding, big CTA verify button, plain text fallback link, 1-hour expiry note
security: OTP stored as SHA-256 hash, not plaintext
Tokens stored as SHA-256(random_bytes(32)) — plaintext issued once, hash stored in DB
ADMIN_TOKEN: hash on startup, compare hashes — never plaintext in memory after init
Rate limit /api/register and /api/reset to 3 req/hour per IP (tower-governor middleware)
dependencies: SendGrid account + API key — BLOCKER, needs jkh
